signals.reasoning: BTC has rebounded sharply, but the live Hormuz blockade headlines still keep the board in a veto regime. Fresh entries are less attractive than simply respecting the event brake.
ETH is participating in the rebound, but the live geopolitical shock still outweighs the local chart improvement. Best to stay neutral under veto.
SOL has improved, but the move still reads more like a rebound in a headline-sensitive tape than a clean durable breakout. Neutral is the disciplined call.
STABLE has a very strong raw squeeze profile, but that alone is not enough to justify a fresh long through a live blockade headline. Best raw long is not the same as best trade now.
VVV is still mostly a short-covering rebound rather than a clean fresh long. With the veto active, that is not enough to justify a trade.
CHILLGUY remains constructive, but it is extended enough that the fresh event risk matters more than the local momentum. Better to stand down.
BOME is too thin and too noisy to trust through a headline-driven regime. No reason to force it.
VIRTUAL is rebounding, but not with a strong enough edge to justify acting through a live geopolitical veto. Watch-only at best.
WCT has squeeze characteristics and improving price action, but the execution quality is still messy and the event brake makes it too easy to get trapped. Keep it neutral.
GOAT remains damaged, but the downside is already too mature and unstable to trust as a fresh short. Weak structure alone is not enough.
JTO still has a real squeeze setup on paper, but the blockade headline keeps this from being a clean actual long. Under veto, it belongs on the watchlist rather than in the buy column.
